authors: Deer TR, Jain S, Hunter C, Chakravarthy K
title: Neurostimulation for Intractable Chronic Pain
study_question: What evidence supports neurostimulation for chronic intractable pain, including mechanisms and clinical efficacy?
population_assessed: Patients with chronic intractable pain, including neuropathic pain syndromes, CRPS, FBSS, and diabetic neuropathy.
follow_up_duration: Variable across reviewed studies; up to 5 years reported.
outcome_measures: Pain relief, quality of life, functional status, disability, medication use.
inclusion_criteria: Chronic intractable pain refractory to conventional medical and interventional therapies.
answer_to_study_question: Neuromodulation generally improves pain and quality of life in chronic intractable pain, though evidence varies by modality.
reasoning: Bibliographic fields showed near-complete agreement, with Anthropic and OpenRouter aligning strongly. This article appears to be a narrative review, not a trial; study fields were merged accordingly. Participant count was set to 0 because the paper reviews prior studies rather than reporting one enrolled cohort, and conflicting counts had lower reliability.
